info_content.text: This app compares vaccination effects and disease effects in a compact visual format.
The app emphasizes interaction and education.
The comparison is about gaining protection through vaccination or through infection, and community vaccination rate affects how likely infection is.

Infection risk also depends on environmental hostility; crowding, geography, carriage, and outbreak context, not just the pathogen itself. The app uses a specific composite variable for this, describing how strongly a disease spreads.
Sources are available through the Sources link, grouped by disease and vaccine. The data, wording, and source links have been manually reviewed.

strings.A short-lived fever or small chickenpox-: A short-lived fever or small chickenpox-like rash can happen after vaccination
strings.An itchy blister-like rash with fever an: An itchy blister-like rash with fever and tiredness that usually lasts about a week
strings.An itchy blister-like rash with mild fev: An itchy blister-like rash with mild fever and tiredness that usually lasts 4 to 7 days
strings.Common short-lived local reaction or fev: Common short-lived local reaction or fever
strings.Common short-lived reaction at the injec: Common short-lived reaction at the injection site
strings.Fever, rash, cough, conjunctivitis, and: Fever, rash, cough, conjunctivitis, and several days of substantial illness
strings.Fever, sore throat, tiredness, nausea, h: Fever, sore throat, tiredness, nausea, headache, or stomach pain for a few days
strings.Illness severe enough to require hospita: Illness severe enough to require hospital care
strings.Infection of the lining around the brain: Infection of the lining around the brain and spinal cord
strings.Less common but still temporary reaction: Less common but still temporary reaction after vaccination
strings.Severe varicella requiring hospital care: Severe varicella requiring hospital care, often from pneumonia or serious bacterial skin infection
strings.Long-term neurologic harm that can follo: Long-term neurologic harm that can follow Hib meningitis
strings.Many rubella infections cause little or: Many rubella infections cause little or no noticeable illness, but can still spread
strings.Most poliovirus infections cause no visi: Most poliovirus infections cause no visible symptoms, but can still spread
strings.Painful swelling of the salivary glands: Painful swelling of the salivary glands with fever, headache, and tiredness
strings.Paralytic polio can lead to breathing fa: Paralytic polio can lead to breathing failure and death
strings.Rare delayed complication such as SSPE: Rare delayed complication such as SSPE
strings.Measles can weaken immune memory and inc: Measles can weaken immune memory and increase vulnerability to other infections for a prolonged period afterward
strings.Rare encephalitis that can become life-t: Rare encephalitis that can become life-threatening
strings.Rare inflammation affecting the lining o: Rare inflammation affecting the lining of the brain and spinal cord or the pancreas
strings.Rare seizure associated with a high feve: Rare seizure associated with a high fever
strings.Rare severe complication that can cause: Rare severe complication that can cause permanent disability
strings.Rare thrombocytopenic purpura or other b: Rare thrombocytopenic purpura or other bleeding complication
strings.Serious bloodstream, throat, lung, bone,: Serious bloodstream, throat, lung, bone, or joint infection requiring hospital treatment
strings.Serious disease requiring hospital care,: Serious disease requiring hospital care, especially in young children
strings.Serious invasive infection of the brain: Serious invasive infection of the brain and spinal cord lining
strings.Severe coughing fits that can last for w: Severe coughing fits that can last for weeks and disrupt sleep, eating, and breathing
strings.Severe varicella requiring hospital care: Severe varicella requiring hospital care, often from pneumonia, dehydration, or serious skin infection
strings.Some mumps infections cause no obvious s: Some mumps infections cause no obvious symptoms, but can still spread
strings.Usually a mild rash illness with fever,: Usually a mild rash illness with fever, sore throat, and swollen glands
strings.Usually a mild rash illness with slight: Usually a mild rash illness with slight fever and swollen glands
strings.Influenza can become life-threatening, e: Influenza can become life-threatening, especially in high-risk groups
strings.Usually a self-limited illness with feve: Usually a self-limited illness with fever, cough, sore throat, body aches, fatigue, and several days of significant symptoms
strings.More serious influenza can lead to dehyd: More serious influenza can lead to dehydration, pneumonia, or worsening of chronic conditions and require hospital care
strings.Many hepatitis A infections, especially: Many hepatitis A infections, especially in younger children, cause no obvious symptoms
strings.Hepatitis A can cause sudden fever, tire: Hepatitis A can cause sudden fever, tiredness, nausea, stomach pain, and jaundice for weeks or months
Hepatitis A can cause sudden fever, tiredness, nausea, stomach pain, and jaundice for weeks or sometimes months
strings.Severe dehydration, weakness, or liver i: Severe dehydration, weakness, or liver inflammation can require hospital care
strings.Severe weakness, dehydration, or liver i: Severe weakness, dehydration, or liver inflammation can require hospital care
strings.In rare cases hepatitis A causes sudden: In rare cases hepatitis A causes sudden liver failure and can be fatal, especially in older people or those with liver disease
strings.Many hepatitis B infections in early lif: Many hepatitis B infections in early life cause no obvious symptoms at first
strings.Many hepatitis B infections, especially: Many hepatitis B infections, especially in infancy and childhood, cause no obvious symptoms at first
strings.When symptoms appear, hepatitis B can ca: When symptoms appear, hepatitis B can cause jaundice, fatigue, nausea, fever, and abdominal pain during the initial infection
strings.Chronic infection with later liver damag: Chronic infection with later liver damage risk
strings.Infection acquired early in life can bec: Infection acquired early in life can become chronic and later lead to cirrhosis or liver cancer
strings.Later fatal liver disease or cancer: Later fatal liver disease or cancer
strings.Some chronic infections later lead to fa: Some chronic infections later lead to fatal liver failure or liver cancer
strings.Local pain, myalgia, or brief fever: Local pain, myalgia, or brief fever
strings.Usually mild pain, muscle aches, or brie: Usually mild pain, muscle aches, or brief fever within a day after vaccination
strings.Most cholera infections are mild or have: Most cholera infections are mild or have no obvious symptoms
strings.Up to 80% of cholera infections cause no: Up to 80% of cholera infections cause no symptoms, and most symptomatic cases remain mild
strings.Severe watery diarrhea and vomiting can: Severe watery diarrhea and vomiting can rapidly lead to life-threatening dehydration
Severe watery diarrhea and vomiting can rapidly cause dehydration
strings.Severe dehydration may require urgent ho: Severe dehydration may require urgent hospital treatment with fluids
strings.Untreated severe cholera can be fatal wi: Untreated severe cholera can be fatal within hours
strings.Usually mild stomach symptoms that pass: Usually mild stomach symptoms that pass quickly
strings.Typhoid can cause prolonged fever, weakn: Typhoid can cause prolonged fever, weakness, stomach pain, headache, and poor appetite
strings.Severe typhoid may require hospital care: Severe typhoid may require hospital care for dehydration, sepsis, or intestinal complications
strings.Untreated typhoid can lead to intestinal: Untreated typhoid can lead to intestinal perforation, sepsis, and death
strings.A small number of people continue carryi: A small number of people continue carrying and spreading the bacteria after recovery
strings.Usually mild fever, pain, or swelling af: Usually mild fever, pain, or swelling after vaccination
strings.Some diphtheria infections can cause few: Some diphtheria infections can cause few or no noticeable symptoms but still spread
strings.A toxin-producing throat infection that: A toxin-producing throat infection that can block breathing and damage the heart and nerves
strings.A toxin-producing throat infection with: A toxin-producing throat infection with sore throat, weakness, swollen glands, and a membrane that can block breathing
strings.Severe toxin effects can damage the hear: Severe toxin effects can damage the heart or nerves and usually require urgent hospital care
strings.Severe illness requiring urgent hospital: Severe illness requiring urgent hospital treatment
strings.Diphtheria can be deadly even with treat: Diphtheria can be deadly even with treatment
strings.Rapid bacterial meningitis or bloodstrea: Rapid bacterial meningitis or bloodstream infection that requires urgent hospital care
strings.Meningococcal disease can become fatal w: Meningococcal disease can become fatal within hours even with treatment
strings.About 1 in 5 survivors can have major di: About 1 in 5 survivors can have major disabilities such as hearing loss, brain damage, seizures, or limb loss after severe infection
strings.Common short-lived effects after meningo: Common short-lived effects after meningococcal vaccination
strings.Some people feel briefly feverish or unw: Some people feel briefly feverish or unwell after vaccination
strings.Common mild-to-moderate injection-site p: Common mild-to-moderate injection-site pain, redness, or swelling that usually resolves within 3 days
strings.Usually mild local soreness, redness, or: Usually mild local soreness, redness, or swelling that lasts a day or two
strings.Headache, fatigue, myalgia, sleepiness,: Headache, fatigue, myalgia, sleepiness, or irritability can happen briefly after vaccination
strings.Less commonly, children may be briefly f: Less commonly, children may be briefly feverish or irritable after vaccination
strings.Extremely rare allergic-type reaction th: Extremely rare allergic-type reaction that may need urgent care
strings.Pneumococcal disease can cause pneumonia: Pneumococcal disease can cause pneumonia, ear infection, or sinus infection and make young children seriously ill
strings.Rotavirus can become life-threatening wh: Rotavirus can become life-threatening when dehydration is severe
strings.Common short-lived soreness or mild ache: Common short-lived soreness or mild aches after vaccination
strings.A brief low-grade fever, tiredness, or b: A brief low-grade fever, tiredness, or body aches can happen after vaccination
strings.Meningitis from pneumococcal disease can: Meningitis from pneumococcal disease can leave permanent hearing loss or neurologic harm
strings.Watery diarrhea, vomiting, fever, and st: Watery diarrhea, vomiting, fever, and stomach pain can last 3 to 8 days and may be the first severe infection
strings.Can cause lifelong paralysis, breathing: Can cause lifelong paralysis, breathing failure, and permanent disability
strings.New muscle weakness, fatigue, and joint: New muscle weakness, fatigue, and joint pain can appear 15 to 40 years after the original infection in some polio survivors
strings.A very rare bowel blockage can happen sh: A very rare bowel blockage can happen shortly after vaccination and needs urgent care
strings.Common mild side effects include sorenes: Common mild side effects include soreness, fever, tiredness, or irritability
strings.Usually mild short-lived soreness, low f: Usually mild short-lived soreness, low fever, irritability, or reduced appetite after vaccination
strings.Dehydration from rotavirus can become se: Dehydration from rotavirus can become severe enough to require hospital treatment
strings.Rotavirus can cause severe dehydration,: Rotavirus can cause severe dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, or acidosis and require hospital treatment
strings.Fever, cough, sore throat, body aches, f: Fever, cough, sore throat, body aches, fatigue, and several days of significant illness
strings.Invasive pneumococcal disease can spread: Invasive pneumococcal disease can spread to the blood or brain and usually requires urgent hospital care
strings.Most babies have no problems, but some m: Most babies have no problems, but some may be briefly irritable or have mild stomach symptoms
strings.Severe influenza can lead to dehydration: Severe influenza can lead to dehydration, pneumonia, or worsening of other health conditions and require hospital care
strings.Severe pneumococcal infection can be dea: Severe pneumococcal infection can be deadly
strings.Severe watery diarrhea, vomiting, fever,: Severe watery diarrhea, vomiting, fever, and stomach pain can last for several days
strings.Some children may be more tired or eat l: Some children may be more tired or eat less for a short time after vaccination
strings.Less common higher fever, vomiting, or d: Less common higher fever, vomiting, or diarrhea after vaccination
strings.Very rare allergic-type reaction that ma: Very rare allergic-type reaction that may need urgent care
strings.Usually mild local soreness, swelling, o: Usually mild local soreness, swelling, or redness after vaccination
strings.Usually mild pain, tenderness, or brief: Usually mild pain, tenderness, or brief fever after vaccination
strings.Usually mild soreness or a brief fever a: Usually mild soreness or a brief fever after vaccination
strings.Headache or tiredness can happen briefly: Headache or tiredness can happen briefly after vaccination
strings.A small minority of acute infections can: A small minority of acute infections can cause sudden life-threatening liver failure

templates.summary.vaccine: {count} out of 100 may notice side effects
